title: streptanthus simulans
intro: Streptanthus simulans, also known as the 'false gumplant', is a rare species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Streptanthus simulans include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used to make teas or infusions, and it can also be used as a poultice to treat skin irritations and wounds.
